author: doll-agent
content:
Plan (Option A — parameterized DType variants):

1. New ferray-core/src/dtype/datetime.rs:
   - TimeUnit enum (Ns, Us, Ms, S, M, H, D) — Copy/Eq/Hash
   - DateTime64(pub i64) newtype + Timedelta64(pub i64) newtype, both Element-sealed
   - NAT: i64 = i64::MIN sentinel
   - PartialEq for DateTime64/Timedelta64 follows NaT-as-NaN semantics (NaT != NaT)
   - Display/Debug with unit suffix

2. dtype/mod.rs:
   - Add DType::DateTime64(TimeUnit) and DType::Timedelta64(TimeUnit) variants
   - Extend size_of (always 8), alignment (8), name (e.g. "datetime64[ns]"), is_signed (true), is_complex (false)
   - Verify Copy/Eq/Hash still derive cleanly (TimeUnit is Copy/Eq/Hash so they should)

3. ferray-io/src/npy/dtype_parse.rs:
   - parse "<M8[ns]" / "<m8[ns]" / etc. descriptors
   - Emit the same in dtype_to_descr

4. ferray-ufunc/src/ops/datetime.rs (new):
   - pub fn isnat<D>(arr: &Array<DateTime64, D>) -> Array<bool, D>
   - Same for Timedelta64

5. Audit and update every match-on-DType site:
   - dtype_to_arrow / arrow_to_dtype in ferray-numpy-interop
   - DynArray dispatch sites
   - DType-aware methods in ferray-ma, ferray-stats, ferray-stride-tricks
   - The internal cast_kind / promote_types logic

6. Tests:
   - DType::DateTime64(Ns) round-trip via NPY
   - isnat correctly identifies NaT sentinel
   - Arithmetic isn't required for the unblock — that's a follow-up

Acceptance: cargo test -p ferray-core -p ferray-io -p ferray-ufunc -p ferray-numpy-interop --features arrow passes; isnat works on DateTime64 / Timedelta64 arrays.
